report_name: Webloc Surveillance System by Cobwebs Technologies Exposed by Citizen Lab Investigation

attack_patterns.name: System Location Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The Webloc system tracks the physical location of mobile devices by leveraging data from the digital advertising ecosystem, specifically the real-time bidding process.

attack_patterns.name: Determine Physical Locations
attack_patterns.description: Webloc is used by government agencies to determine the physical location and track the real-time movements of individuals via their mobile devices.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Host Information
attack_patterns.description: The Webloc system tracks devices by querying their unique mobile advertising ID, which is broadcast by apps during the ad bidding process.

attack_patterns.name: Search Open Technical Databases
attack_patterns.description: Webloc queries a vast database of location data harvested from the digital advertising ecosystem, which acts as an open technical database of device locations and advertising IDs.

tools.name: Webloc
tools.description: A global surveillance tool developed by Cobwebs Technologies that leverages data from the digital advertising ecosystem to track the location of up to 500 million mobile devices worldwide.

identities.name: Citizen Lab
identities.description: The University of Toronto's research lab that uncovered and reported on the Webloc surveillance system.

identities.name: Cobwebs Technologies
identities.description: An Israeli web intelligence (WEBINT) firm that originally developed the Webloc surveillance tool.

identities.name: Penlink
identities.description: The successor company to Cobwebs Technologies, which acquired it in July 2023 and now sells the Webloc tool.

identities.name: Hungarian domestic intelligence
identities.description: A known government client and user of the Webloc surveillance system.

identities.name: El Salvador national police
identities.description: A known government client and user of the Webloc surveillance system.

identities.name: U.S. law enforcement departments
identities.description: A class of known government clients and users of the Webloc surveillance system.

infrastructure.name: Webloc Surveillance Database
infrastructure.description: A vast database of location data and advertising IDs for up to 500 million devices, harvested from the digital advertising ecosystem and queried by the Webloc tool.
